title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a homer and went 2-for-4 with three RBIs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea.
Yoshida's third-inning solo home run came off Jo Byeong-Hyeon, contributing to Japan's lead at the Tokyo Dome.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game in Japan, wearing a Dodgers uniform with his wife.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Roslindale native Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, 25, is running her first Boston Marathon with Team Eye and Ear in 2026.
She is running to support Mass Eye and Ear, where she has been a patient for nearly 10 years with eye conditions.
Aboujaoude-Quirk teaches in Boston Public Schools and aims to fundraise for the hospital's research and specialized emergency care.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler has been promoted to ESPN/ABC's top NBA broadcast team for the 2025-26 season.
Legler joined ESPN in 2000 and will now call NBA Finals games with Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson.
Legler praised the Boston Celtics' performance and is eager to see Jayson Tatum's return from injury.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics with 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists after a 298-day injury recovery.
Maine native Cooper Flagg made his NBA debut at TD Garden, scoring 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists for the Mavericks.
Cooper Flagg's Garden debut drew a large crowd, and he received a warm ovation despite being an opposing player.

title: Charles Barkley impressed by Jayson Tatum in return, but doesn’t view Celtics as favorites in East
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Charles Barkley remains skeptical about the Celtics' chances in the East, favoring the Cavaliers after their trade for James Harden.
Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics lineup on Friday after an Achilles injury, contributing 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists.
Boston Celtics hold a strong recent record with 22 wins since January, tying the NBA's best streak alongside the San Antonio Spurs.

title: Mass. man accused of impersonating an Army veteran for more than 30 years
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: James D. Sommers from Pittsfield charged with impersonating an Army veteran to receive government benefits for over 30 years.
Sommers allegedly stole the identity of a real veteran, receiving thousands in Social Security and VA medical benefits.
Sommers amassed criminal convictions in New York under the victim's name; faces up to five years in prison if convicted.

title: Watch: Body cam video shows officer rescuing man, 78, from burning truck
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Easton police officer Dean Soucie and two bystanders rescued a 78-year-old man from a burning truck on Route 138.
The incident occurred on Feb. 27 after a three-vehicle crash involving a Mack dump truck, a Ford F-350, and a Toyota Camry.
The rescued driver, Francis Leverone, was hospitalized with serious but non-life-threatening injuries, including a dislocated hip.

title: US attorney going after money forfeited by Mass. resident in romance crypto fraud scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Federal prosecutors seek to reclaim over $327,000 in cryptocurrency linked to a Massachusetts romance and investment scam.
The scam involved a fake relationship where the victim was convinced to invest in crypto, later laundered via Tether.
Authorities tracked the funds to unhosted wallets seized in August 2025, connected to alleged money laundering activities.

title: Jayson Tatum’s fashion also makes a return in his comeback from Achilles tear
author: LaDonna LaGuerre
bullets: Jayson Tatum showcased a fashion mix of Parisian luxury, '90s nostalgia, and street swagger before Friday's Celtics game.
In his return from an Achilles tear, Tatum played 27 minutes, achieving a double-double with threes and dunks.
Tatum's pregame outfit featured Lanvin denim, a collared shirt, Timberlands, and 'Bruce Lee' Jordan sneakers.

title: She survived a brain tumor. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Tracy Haisley, 50, from Columbia, SC, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon after surviving a brain tumor and recovery.
Haisley aims to honor her journey and inspire others by raising awareness for public libraries as a member of the Boston Public Library team.
Haisley has served on the Board of the Richland Library for nearly 15 years, advocating for literacy and digital access.
